commit.message:
test: extend ENA poll to 20 min, add GetConsoleOutput at 5 min mark

The 10-minute timeout was too short for the two-boot sequence:
  first boot (no ENA) → detect extra-nic.npk → install → reboot
  second boot (with ENA) → DHCP → WebFig on port 80

Raised timeout to 20 min and interval to 20s.

Also added GetConsoleOutput dump at ~5 minutes for diagnostics.
ARM64 CHR kernel uses ttyAMA0 so console will likely be empty
(Graviton captures ttyS0), but the call confirms the instance is
alive and may catch any UEFI-level messages.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: remove 'from __future__ import annotations' to fix slowapi wrapper resolution

With future annotations, type hints become strings that FastAPI resolves
via the function's __globals__. slowapi's limiter.limit() decorator wraps
endpoints with a function whose __globals__ belongs to the slowapi module,
where request/response model classes don't exist. This caused 422 errors
on POST endpoints in CI (FastAPI 0.115.12 + Python 3.11).

Removing the future import makes annotations actual type objects, bypassing
the __globals__ lookup entirely.

commit.message:
Don't include inferred 'who' in the involves list (#63)

The involved list is used by the ?involves=true filter to show PRs
a user has explicitly participated in (authored, reviewed, commented,
or was requested to review).  It was also including the 'who' field,
which is an inferred suggestion of who should act next.  This caused
maintainers who happen to have write access (e.g. from merging a few
of their own PRs) to see every unowned PR in their involves view,
even in repos they don't actively review.

Remove the who -> involvedSet propagation so that involves reflects
only actual participation.

commit.message:
fix(device): add TX ring silence guard to prevent stale-data pops

ExternalBuffer uses unconditional_read=true which bypasses the ring's
readable_pos freshness check. After the first ring revolution (~682 ms)
positions beyond write_pos hold old audio data.  When write_pos stalls
during MXWANI8 silence suppression (block==0), TX reads those stale
positions → pop on every silence→audio transition.

Fix A (silence): when block==0, zero-fill ring positions from write_pos
up to tx_current_pos + LEAD_SAMPLES so TX always reads clean silence
instead of old audio from the previous revolution.

Fix B (gap resumption): when block > 2×LEAD (a gap just ended), snap
write_pos to tx_current_pos + LEAD_SAMPLES before writing.  Without
this, the large catch-up batch advances write_pos far ahead of TX,
causing latency runaway until the ring overflows.

tx_current_pos is read from current_ts_cb (set by flows_tx.rs) and
adjusted by ptp_at_poll to convert absolute PTP timestamp to ring offset.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

ruff formatting.

before `git add -A`. This ensures every agent commit passes formatting
checks regardless of CLI output.

Failures are suppressed — the worktree might contain non-Python files,
and formatting shouldn't block commits.
